title: Solo Backpacking Across Northern Vietnam
title_zh: 獨自背包橫越越南北部

all.en: Mei had saved for two years to take a solo backpacking trip across northern Vietnam.
all.zh: 美儀存了兩年的錢，準備獨自背包旅行穿越越南北部。

all.en: She landed in Hanoi at midnight, tired but excited to begin her journey.
all.zh: 她在午夜抵達河內，雖然疲憊卻興奮地準備展開旅程。

all.en: The streets were still alive with scooters, neon lights, and the smell of grilled meat.
all.zh: 街道上仍充滿摩托車、霓虹燈光與烤肉的香氣。

all.en: Her hostel owner greeted her warmly and offered a small bowl of hot noodle soup.
all.zh: 民宿老闆熱情地迎接她，並端上一碗熱騰騰的麵湯。

all.en: The next morning, she wandered through the Old Quarter with her camera in hand.
all.zh: 隔天早上，她手拿相機在老城區裡四處漫遊。

all.en: An elderly woman selling flowers smiled and let Mei take her portrait.
all.zh: 一位賣花的老婦人微笑著讓美儀為她拍下肖像。

all.en: By afternoon, Mei had joined a free walking tour led by a local university student.
all.zh: 下午時，美儀加入了一場由當地大學生帶領的免費徒步導覽。

all.en: He explained the history of the French colonial buildings with quiet pride.
all.zh: 他帶著靜謐的自豪解說法國殖民建築的歷史。

all.en: That evening she tried egg coffee for the first time and fell in love with the taste.
all.zh: 那天傍晚她第一次嘗試蛋咖啡，立刻愛上那獨特的味道。

all.en: Two days later she boarded an overnight bus heading toward Sapa in the mountains.
all.zh: 兩天後她搭上一輛夜班巴士，前往山區的沙壩。

all.en: The bus climbed steep curves while passengers slept under thin cotton blankets.
all.zh: 巴士爬上陡峭彎道，乘客們蓋著薄棉被沉睡。

all.en: She woke to a sunrise painting the rice terraces in soft shades of gold.
all.zh: 她醒來時看見日出將梯田染上柔和的金色光輝。

all.en: A Hmong woman named Su offered to guide her through the surrounding villages.
all.zh: 一位名叫蘇的赫蒙族婦女主動提議帶她遊覽周邊村落。

all.en: They walked along narrow paths between fields where buffalo grazed quietly.
all.zh: 她們沿著田間小徑而行，水牛在一旁靜靜吃草。

all.en: Su shared stories about her four children and her dreams for their education.
all.zh: 蘇分享了她四個孩子的故事，以及對他們教育的期望。

all.en: Mei was invited to stay overnight in Su's wooden home above a small stream.
all.zh: 美儀被邀請到蘇位於小溪上方的木屋過夜。

all.en: Dinner was simple rice, fresh greens, and pork cooked over an open fire.
all.zh: 晚餐是簡單的米飯、新鮮青菜，以及在火上烤熟的豬肉。

all.en: After the meal, Su's daughter shyly practiced English phrases with their guest.
all.zh: 用餐後，蘇的女兒害羞地與這位客人練習英文句子。

all.en: Mei taught her a few simple words in Mandarin and they laughed together.
all.zh: 美儀教她幾個簡單的中文單字，兩人一起開懷大笑。

all.en: The next morning Mei hugged the family goodbye and promised to write letters.
all.zh: 隔天早上美儀擁抱這家人道別，並答應會寫信給他們。

all.en: From Sapa she traveled east toward Ha Long Bay by train and ferry.
all.zh: 她從沙壩搭火車與渡輪，向東前往下龍灣。

all.en: The limestone islands rose from the sea like silent green giants.
all.zh: 石灰岩島嶼從海面升起，像沉默的綠色巨人。

all.en: She joined a small boat tour with travelers from Spain, Korea, and Australia.
all.zh: 她參加了一場小船遊覽，同行的有來自西班牙、韓國與澳洲的旅客。

all.en: Together they kayaked into hidden caves and swam in cool emerald water.
all.zh: 他們一起划獨木舟進入隱密洞穴，並在清涼的翡翠色水中游泳。

all.en: That night, the group shared stories on the deck under a sky full of stars.
all.zh: 那晚一群人在甲板上分享故事，頭頂上是滿天星斗。

all.en: Mei realized she no longer felt afraid of traveling alone in a strange country.
all.zh: 美儀意識到自己已不再害怕獨自在異國旅行。

all.en: She continued south to Hue, where she explored ancient royal tombs by bicycle.
all.zh: 她繼續南下到順化，騎著腳踏車探索古代皇陵。

all.en: A sudden rainstorm forced her to take shelter under the roof of a roadside temple.
all.zh: 一場突如其來的暴雨迫使她躲進路邊一座寺廟的屋簷下。

all.en: A monk offered her hot tea and they sat watching the rain in peaceful silence.
all.zh: 一位僧人遞給她熱茶，兩人靜靜坐著看雨，氣氛安詳。

all.en: By the end of three weeks, her notebook was full of names, sketches, and addresses.
all.zh: 三週結束時，她的筆記本裡滿是姓名、速寫與地址。

all.en: Her backpack was heavier with small gifts but her heart felt lighter than ever.
all.zh: 她的背包因小禮物而變得更重，內心卻比以往任何時候都輕盈。

all.en: On the flight home, she stared out the window and replayed each encounter.
all.zh: 回程的飛機上，她望著窗外，腦中回放每一次相遇。

all.en: She understood that the kindest moments had come from strangers asking nothing in return.
all.zh: 她明白最溫暖的時刻都來自不求回報的陌生人。

all.en: Mei promised herself she would return one day, perhaps to volunteer in Sapa.
all.zh: 美儀答應自己有一天會再回來，也許是去沙壩當志工。

all.en: For now, she carried the memory of warm rice, soft rain, and quiet friendship home.
all.zh: 此刻，她帶著溫熱米飯、細雨與靜默友誼的回憶回家。

all.en: Back in Taipei, Mei printed her favorite photos and built a small album by hand.
all.zh: 回到台北後，美儀印出她最喜歡的照片，並親手做了一本小相簿。

all.en: She wrote her first letter to Su's family that very weekend at her kitchen table.
all.zh: 她就在那個週末於廚房餐桌前寫下了給蘇一家人的第一封信。

all.en: Inside the envelope she tucked photos of the children and a few sheets of pretty stickers.
all.zh: 她在信封裡塞進孩子們的照片，以及幾張漂亮的貼紙。

all.en: A reply arrived two months later, written carefully in mixed Vietnamese and English.
all.zh: 兩個月後回信寄來了，是用越南文與英文混合著仔細寫成的。

all.en: Su's daughter shared that she had been accepted into a new local English program.
all.zh: 蘇的女兒分享她已經被當地一個新的英文課程錄取。

all.en: Mei wept reading those words and immediately decided to fund a small annual scholarship.
all.zh: 美儀讀到那些字句時哭了，並立刻決定每年資助一筆小小的獎學金。

all.en: She set up monthly transfers and asked a local nonprofit to manage the simple paperwork.
all.zh: 她設定了每月轉帳，並請一個當地的非營利組織協助處理簡單的文件手續。

all.en: Friends back home heard about the project and began contributing small amounts as well.
all.zh: 她在台灣的朋友聽說了這個計畫，也開始陸續捐出小額善款。

all.en: Within a year, three other village children had joined the scholarship program too.
all.zh: 不到一年，村裡另外三個孩子也加入了這個獎學金計畫。

all.en: Mei booked her return ticket to Sapa for the following spring with quiet, certain joy.
all.zh: 美儀以一份寧靜而篤定的喜悅，訂下了隔年春天重返沙壩的機票。

all.en: Mei flew back to Sapa the following April carrying gifts and her advanced camera equipment.
all.zh: 美儀在隔年四月飛回沙壩，帶著禮物與她進階的攝影器材。

all.en: Su welcomed her at the bus station with her two oldest children running ahead in excitement.
all.zh: 蘇在公車站迎接她，兩個較大的孩子興奮地跑在前頭。

all.en: The wooden house had grown a small extension thanks to the scholarship and other community donations.
all.zh: 那棟木屋多了一間小擴建，感謝獎學金以及其他社區的捐助。

all.en: Mei volunteered at the village school, teaching English songs and helping children practice short conversations.
all.zh: 美儀在村裡的學校當志工，教英文歌並幫孩子們練習簡短對話。

all.en: She photographed the daily rhythm of the school for a small fundraising photo book project.
all.zh: 她拍攝了學校的日常作息，作為一本小型募款照片書計畫。

all.en: Friends back home preordered enough copies to fund another two years of the scholarship program.
all.zh: 台灣的朋友預訂的本數，足以再支應獎學金計畫兩年的經費。

all.en: Su's daughter won a regional English speech contest with a quiet, beautifully spoken personal story.
all.zh: 蘇的女兒以一段安靜而動人的個人故事贏得了區域英文演講比賽。

all.en: Mei watched the video late one night and cried with tea cooling slowly in her hand.
all.zh: 美儀某個深夜看著那段影片，手中的茶逐漸放涼，眼淚也跟著流下。

all.en: The local nonprofit invited Mei to join their advisory board for rural education in northern Vietnam.
all.zh: 當地的非營利組織邀請美儀加入他們在越南北部偏鄉教育的顧問委員會。

all.en: She accepted, knowing that one delayed bus and one warm bowl had truly changed her whole life.
all.zh: 她接受了，因為她明白一班延誤的巴士和一碗熱湯，真的徹底改變了她的人生。

all.en: On her next trip to Sapa, Mei rented a tiny scooter and learned to navigate the muddy mountain lanes.
all.zh: 下次去沙壩時，美儀租了一台小機車，學會在泥濘的山間小路上騎行。

all.en: She often joked that her Mandarin had stayed the same but her Vietnamese vocabulary had quadrupled.
all.zh: 她常開玩笑說，她的中文沒什麼進步，但越南語單字量倒是翻了四倍。

all.en: Su's youngest son started calling Mei his Taiwan auntie whenever she video-called the family.
all.zh: 蘇最小的兒子每次美儀視訊回家時，都開始稱她為「台灣阿姨」。

all.en: Mei taught the village kids how to make hand-drip coffee using the equipment she brought from Taipei.
all.zh: 美儀教村裡的孩子們用她從台北帶來的器具沖手沖咖啡。

all.en: She started a small online shop selling Sapa-grown coffee beans with proceeds going back to the village.
all.zh: 她開了一個小型網路商店，販售沙壩當地的咖啡豆，收益回饋給村莊。

all.en: Her Taipei apartment slowly filled with souvenirs, batik fabrics, and photos pinned to a corkboard wall.
all.zh: 她台北的公寓漸漸塞滿了紀念品、蠟染布料，以及釘在軟木板牆上的照片。

all.en: On rainy weekends, she would brew Vietnamese coffee at home and write long emails back to Sapa.
all.zh: 下雨的週末，她會在家泡越南咖啡，並寫長長的電子郵件回沙壩。

all.en: Su's daughter eventually applied to a university in Hanoi and asked Mei to be her reference.
all.zh: 蘇的女兒後來申請了河內的一所大學，並請美儀當她的推薦人。

all.en: Mei wrote the recommendation letter at her kitchen table, smiling at how full life had become.
all.zh: 美儀在她的廚房餐桌前寫下那封推薦信，微笑著想著生活如今變得多麼充實。

all.en: Years later, the scholarship she had quietly started was supporting a dozen rural Vietnamese children.
all.zh: 多年後，她當初悄悄發起的獎學金，已支持著十多位越南鄉村兒童。

all.en: I saved for two years to backpack alone across northern Vietnam.
all.zh: 我存了兩年錢，要獨自背包橫越越南北部。

all.en: My flight landed in Hanoi at midnight, and I was both tired and thrilled.
all.zh: 班機半夜降落河內，我又累又興奮。

all.en: The streets were still buzzing with scooters and the smell of grilled meat.
all.zh: 街上仍滿是機車和烤肉的香味。

all.en: A street vendor refused my money and simply wished me a safe trip.
all.zh: 一個路邊攤販不肯收我的錢，只祝我旅途平安。

all.en: Traveling solo taught me to trust strangers more than I expected.
all.zh: 獨自旅行教我比想像中更願意信任陌生人。

all.en: I rode a creaky overnight train up into the cool northern mountains.
all.zh: 我搭著嘎吱作響的夜車，駛進涼爽的北方山區。

all.en: Getting lost in the rice terraces turned out to be the best part.
all.zh: 在梯田裡迷路，竟然成了最棒的部分。

all.en: A local family invited me to share a hot bowl of pho for free.
all.zh: 一戶當地人家請我免費共享一碗熱騰騰的河粉。

all.en: The kindest moments came from people who wanted nothing in return.
all.zh: 最溫暖的時刻，來自那些不求回報的人。

all.en: That trip changed me so much that I started a small scholarship back home.
all.zh: 那趟旅程改變我太多，回國後我設立了一個小小的獎學金。
